title: olearia rugosa subsp. allenderae
intro: Olearia rugosa subsp. allenderae, also known as Allender's tree daisy, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the daisy family, native to Western Australia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been used to treat a range of health issues. The plant is said to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, soothing skin irritations, and exhibiting antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, Olearia rugosa subsp. allenderae is typically used as a tincture, infused oil, or dried herb to make teas, with caution and under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
